title: Antholyza gawleri
intro: Antholyza gawleri, also known as Anarthria or Black Grass-lily, is a small perennial plant native to eastern Australia, characterized by its distinctive black flowers and long, narrow leaves. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, relieve fever, and soothe respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Antholyza gawleri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
